summary: Concurrent edit conflict: tool.ts was modified by another session between tool-telemetry edits. My imports and telemetry code were overwritten. File went from 293→323 lines (added CacheKey import and cache-related code).
detail: tool.ts was modified concurrently. My telemetry changes (imports for AppFileSystem, InstanceState, path; startTime capture; telemetry tap/tapError) were applied initially but the file on disk now shows a different state with CacheKey import added. Must re-apply on top of current state.

summary: Pre-existing cache pipeline bug breaks all tool execute() calls. The cache Effect.gen wrapper added by concurrent session causes Pipeable.js TypeError in any test that calls execute().
detail: Cache code (lines 190-213) wraps original execute in Effect.gen with Effect.annotateCurrentSpan calls. Even when cache service is None, the execute call crashes with 'args[1] is not a function' in Pipeable.js. Existing tool-define.test.ts tests 4-5 also fail (tests 1-3 pass since they only test init()). Root cause is somewhere in the cache gen composition — likely Effect.annotateCurrentSpan interaction with the nested gen scope. Tool telemetry code is correct and compiles cleanly.
